calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kai Wessels for and with Kenneth Garzo.

Mr. Wessels addressed the issues raised in the Probate Examiner's notes stating that the "interest credit received" was from the Citibank accounts, that $10,201.15 is deducted from the $32,407.50 amount for condo expenses (monthly fee and repairs), and that the recalculation from the intestate Heir to Dori Van Lastyne is $22,206.35. Mr. Wessels also explained difficulty, services rendered and time spent to support fees. The accounting petition was taken under submission. Mr. Wessels is to file a brief declaration of the expenses.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-25-2014 Continued To Jul-09-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-28-2013 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-07-2013 Continued To Oct-28-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kai Wessels for Kenneth Garzo (Petitioner). Richard Scott for Beneficiary.

The Clerk informed counsel that this matter was continued to October 28, 2013 by the Probate Examiner. Mr. Wessels stated that he came from San Jose and that Mr. Scott came from Los Angeles for the hearing. Furthermore, he stated that he has file the Will and that he has a proof of holographic instrument and there is no reason for further delay. The Court indicated that the order will be reviewed between now and October 28, 2013.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Aug-26-2013 Continued To Oct-07-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kai Wessels for Kenneth Garzo (Petitioner). Richard Scott (Attorney in the Los Angeles matter) also present.

Counsel reported that the Los Angeles case was dismissed, that it will take 8 to 12 weeks to obtain the dismissal, that they have a copy of the order transferring the will and that the Client is willing to proceed in San Francisco. The Court noted the numerous continuances and advised Counsel that it will not take jurisdiction over the matter unless it receives the original will. The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed was continued to October 7, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to provide said documents.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jun-24-2013 Continued To Aug-26-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kai Wessels for Kenneth Garzo.

Mr. Wessels stated that it will take the Los Angeles Court four to six weeks to sign the order transferring the original will to the San Francisco Court. The Petition for Letters of Administration With Will Annexed was continued to August 26, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jun-03-2013 Continued To Jun-24-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Apr-24-2013 Continued To Jun-03-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Mar-13-2013 Continued To May-20-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kai Wessels for Kenneth Garzo.

Mr. Wessels stated that the Los Angeles case was dismissed with prejudice, that he has a certified copy of the Los Angeles will and argued that the original will should not be required when a probate is in another state. The Court clarified that Los Angeles is in the State of California and continued the Petition for Letters of Administration to May 20, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Wessels to file a declaration setting forth that the Los Angeles case is dismissed, a copy of the dismissal and the original will.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.
